title: Parallax Write Instruction (m-unit-work)
description: The canonical, language-neutral write-instruction vocabulary a unit of work buffers and flushes (m-unit-work) — the write-side analogue of `operation.schema.json`. A write from any frontend lowers into exactly one instruction of one of two shapes: a `keyed` instruction (`mutation` + `entity` + `rows`, the flat attribute-named write input) or a `predicate` instruction (`mutation` + `target` {entity, predicate} + `assignments`, the set-based write). Embedded predicates are canonical `m-op-algebra` nodes, legal here because `m-unit-work` already depends on `m-op-algebra`; this schema treats a predicate as an opaque object and defers its validation to `operation.schema.json`, exactly as `compatibility-case.schema.json` does for a `writeTarget.predicate`. The instant surface is AXIS-EXPLICIT: business bounds are named uniformly `businessFrom` / `businessTo` (never the corpus's overloaded `at` or the split `businessAt` / `until` authoring aliases). Every bounded (`*Until`) operation carries BOTH business bounds — its window is `[businessFrom, businessTo)` (m-bitemp-write). The PROCESSING instant is deliberately NOT an instruction field — it is harness / Clock-Strategy supplied context bound at flush (ADR 0010), so no caller-facing instruction shape can smuggle one in. The framework-owned transaction OBSERVATION (optimistic version / observed `in_z`) is likewise NOT an instruction field: it is attached per materialized row at flush (ADR 0013), so the reserved control keys `observedVersion` and `observedInZ` are explicitly FORBIDDEN on a durable write row — an observation cannot round-trip as instruction state. `compatibility-case.schema.json` keeps the corpus's `at` / `businessAt` / `until` spellings as authoring aliases of these canonical names (the corpus-wide re-authoring to the canonical spellings is deferred), and references these `$defs` rather than redefining them. SERDE ROUND-TRIP CONTRACT: a conforming implementation MUST round-trip every instruction through this canonical form losslessly — `serialize(deserialize(x)) == x` (JSON and YAML) — the write-side of the `m-op-algebra` serde contract.

keyedWriteInstruction.description: A keyed write: a mutation on one entity carrying the flat attribute-named neutral write input (`rows`). The MVP non-temporal / audit-only surface is `insert` / `update` / `delete` / `terminate`; the bounded full-bitemporal rectangle split adds `insertUntil` / `updateUntil` / `terminateUntil`. A bounded `*Until` mutation carries BOTH explicit business bounds `businessFrom` and `businessTo` (its window is `[businessFrom, businessTo)`); a plain (unbounded) temporal mutation carries only `businessFrom` (its window runs `[businessFrom, infinity)`). Deferred extension verbs (`cascadeDelete`, business-only, the MAY-tier recovery / increment mutations) are added to `keyedMutation` when their slices claim them, without reshaping the instruction.

rows.description: The neutral write input: one or more flat attribute-named rows the mutation operates on (`$defs/writeRow`). A generating implementation classifies each field by its metamodel role and derives the emitted column list and ORDER from `columnOrder(entity)`.

businessFrom.description: The BUSINESS-axis lower bound of the mutation's valid-time window (`from_z`). Present on a temporal (audit-only / business / bitemporal) mutation, and REQUIRED on every bounded `*Until` mutation (whose window is `[businessFrom, businessTo)`). The corpus authoring alias for the business lower bound on a business-only step is `businessAt`.

businessTo.description: The BUSINESS-axis exclusive upper bound of a bounded (`*Until`) mutation's valid-time window (`thru_z`). Present ONLY on `insertUntil` / `updateUntil` / `terminateUntil`, and always PAIRED with `businessFrom` (the window is `[businessFrom, businessTo)`); the corpus authoring alias is `until`. A plain (unbounded) temporal mutation omits it — its window runs `[businessFrom, infinity)`.

allOf.description: A bounded `*Until` mutation is over `[businessFrom, businessTo)` (m-bitemp-write), so it MUST carry BOTH business bounds; a plain (unbounded) mutation MUST NOT carry `businessTo`.

predicateWriteInstruction.description: A predicate-selected (set-based) write: a mutation on every row of `target.entity` matching `target.predicate`, with `assignments` on the update forms. This is the set-based analogue of the keyed instruction; `m-batch-write` / `m-opt-lock` decide whether it lowers readless (unversioned non-temporal) or materializes per-row (versioned / temporal). Assignment list order is DATA order only — descriptor `columnOrder(entity)` determines the emitted SET columns and their binds.

businessFrom.description: The BUSINESS-axis lower bound for a plain or bounded temporal predicate write (`from_z`). REQUIRED on every bounded `*Until` predicate write (whose window is `[businessFrom, businessTo)`). The corpus authoring alias is `businessFrom` already (predicate writes were authored axis-explicit from the start, COR-35).

businessTo.description: The BUSINESS-axis exclusive upper bound of a bounded (`*Until`) predicate write's window (`thru_z`). Present ONLY on `updateUntil` / `terminateUntil`, and always PAIRED with `businessFrom` (the window is `[businessFrom, businessTo)`); the corpus authoring alias is `until`.

allOf.description: The assignment-bearing update forms (`update` / `updateUntil`) MUST carry `assignments`; `delete` / `terminate` / `terminateUntil` MUST NOT.
A bounded `*Until` predicate write is over `[businessFrom, businessTo)` (m-bitemp-write), so it MUST carry BOTH business bounds; the unbounded forms MUST NOT carry `businessTo`.

writeTarget.description: The exact entity a predicate-selected write begins from plus its bare `m-op-algebra` predicate. The predicate is validated against `operation.schema.json` and the descriptor-dependent scope / bare-predicate rules separately (it must be a bare write predicate, never a result modifier).

writeAssignment.description: One ordered predicate-write assignment. `attr` is a qualified descriptor attribute or top-level value-object reference and `value` is a neutral literal: a scalar / null for an attribute or the complete object / array document for a value object. Duplicate and unassignable (framework-owned / PK / relationship) references are rejected by model-aware validation.

writeRow.description: A flat attribute-named DURABLE write row — the neutral write input, same vocabulary as a fixture row. Every key is an entity ATTRIBUTE name or a top-level VALUE-OBJECT name (a business/developer name like `id` / `orderedOn` / `address`, never a physical column). The framework-owned transaction OBSERVATION is NOT durable instruction state: the reserved control keys `observedVersion` (the optimistic-lock version) and `observedInZ` (the observed processing-from) are attached per materialized row at flush (m-opt-lock, ADR 0013), so they are explicitly FORBIDDEN here — the advance (`observedVersion + 1`) and the `and version = ?` / `and in_z = ?` gates are derived at flush, never authored onto the instruction. A scalar attribute's value is a scalar literal, an explicit `null`, or a one-key DB-computed marker (`{ computed: "maxPlusOne" }` or `{ increment: <n> }`); a value-object field's value is the WHOLE embedded composite document (a JSON object, or a JSON array of objects for a `many` value object), or `null`. A generating implementation classifies each field by its metamodel role and derives the emitted column list and ORDER from `columnOrder(entity)`, binding a value object atomically as one document in its columnOrder position; a marker-shaped value is a DB-computed marker vs a value-object document by MODEL ROLE, not by the value's shape — so the row cannot be `additionalProperties: false`.

not.description: The framework-owned transaction observation is not an instruction field: neither the optimistic version (`observedVersion`) nor the observed processing-from (`observedInZ`) may ride on the durable write row (m-unit-work, ADR 0013). They are flush-time context on `compatibility-case.schema.json`'s materialization row, never here.

writeRowValue.description: The value a neutral write-row field carries, classified by the field's metamodel role (resolved from `columnOrder(entity)`, NOT from the value's shape): a scalar literal / explicit `null` for a scalar attribute; a one-key DB-computed marker (`writeComputedMarker`) for a computed scalar column; or the WHOLE embedded value-object document — a JSON object for a `one` member, a JSON array of objects for a `many` member — bound atomically in its columnOrder position. The branches are NON-exclusive (`anyOf`): a marker-shaped value-object document validates via both the marker and the document branch, and the model role decides. This is the single shared value vocabulary `compatibility-case.schema.json`'s flush-context write row references rather than redefining.

anyOf.description: A value-object document (m-value-object): a value-object-named field binds the WHOLE embedded composite atomically as one document value in its columnOrder position — a JSON object for a `one` value object, a JSON array of objects for a `many` one.

writeComputedMarker.description: A one-key DB-COMPUTED marker in a neutral write row: the attribute's value is derived by the database rather than authored as a literal bind, so the generating implementation emits the strategy's SQL fragment. Applies ONLY to a scalar-attribute column (a value-object column's value is always the literal document even when marker-shaped, disambiguated by metamodel role, not by shape).

computed.description: The pk-gen `max` strategy: the column is emitted as `coalesce(max(col), ?) + ?` — the computation folds into the INSERT's SQL, so a `max`-strategy insert stays statically derivable.

increment.description: A self-referential advance emitted as `col = col + ?` (e.g. a sequence registry's `next_val`); the integer is the amount added.

instant.description: A business-axis instant: an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp string, or the open-bound sentinel `infinity`.

entityName.description: An exact concrete descriptor entity name (the position a write begins from).

keyedMutation.description: The keyed write mutation surface: the MVP non-temporal / audit-only verbs (`insert` / `update` / `delete` / `terminate`) plus the full-bitemporal bounded rectangle split (`insertUntil` / `updateUntil` / `terminateUntil`). Deferred extension verbs are added here when their slices claim them. `compatibility-case.schema.json`'s buffered keyed write references this enum rather than redefining it.

predicateMutation.description: The predicate-selected (set-based) write mutation surface: the update / delete / terminate verbs plus the bounded `updateUntil` / `terminateUntil` (a set-based write has no `insert` form — there is no predicate to select rows that do not yet exist). `compatibility-case.schema.json`'s `predicateWrite` references this enum rather than redefining it.
